design_calls.question: Which of the three options (custom driver / merge=ours + post-merge hook / documented protocol only) is the proper fix?
design_calls.recommendation: Option (i): a custom keep-ours merge driver named `generated`, declared in .gitattributes and installed into repo-local git config by an idempotent `ensure` subcommand, combined with the already-mandated post-merge regen (handoff HANDOFF.md lines 62-66: only the integrator runs the regen scripts). Options (ii) and (iii) are rejected.
design_calls.rationale: Option (ii) is a false alternative: git has NO built-in attribute-level `merge=ours` driver (built-ins are text/binary/union only), so `merge=ours` needs the exact same per-clone `[merge "ours"] driver = true` config as option (i) while losing the loud notice; `merge=union` corrupts JSON/py; and a post-merge hook has a WORSE deployment story than config (.git/hooks is per-clone AND currently empty in this very clone — pre-commit is not installed here, verified). Option (iii) does not kill the conflict class, it just documents hand-resolving it every wave. Empirically verified on this Windows/Git-Bash box: an attributes-declared driver that is UNDEFINED in config degrades to a normal text merge -> conflict (exactly today's behavior — the undeployed state is loudly-failing, never silently wrong), and a defined keep-ours driver merges cleanly keeping ours.

design_calls.question: How does per-clone git config deploy across worktrees and agent clones, given .git/config is not versioned and git refuses to auto-apply repo-provided config?
design_calls.recommendation: Self-deploy through the regen entry point: `scripts/merge_generated.py ensure` idempotently writes `merge.generated.driver` + `merge.generated.name` into repo-local config (git config, not a hand-edit), and WS1's `scripts/regen_all.py` calls it at the top of every run. Fresh clones that have never run regen degrade loudly (ordinary conflict), never silently.
design_calls.rationale: Verified empirically: worktrees created with `git worktree add` share the common git dir's config (`git rev-parse --git-common-dir` in the worktree points at the main clone's .git; `git config --get merge.generated.driver` resolves from the worktree) — so ONE ensure per clone covers every agent worktree, which is the actual swarm topology (EnterWorktree uses worktrees of this clone). The integrator MUST run regen_all after every merge wave anyway (repo law since the 07-12 handoff), so the driver installs itself on first use in any clone without violating git's no-auto-config security rule — the user is already executing repo code when running regen. A `check` subcommand exits nonzero when the driver is missing/stale, giving CI/doctor a hook if ever wanted.

design_calls.question: Which files get `merge=generated`?
design_calls.recommendation: ONLY files that are 100% regenerable pure functions of the registry: `src/hpc_agent/operations.json`, `src/hpc_agent/schemas/*.json` (245 files, 90 commits since 07-01), `src/hpc_agent/cli/_verb_module_map.py`, `docs/generated/*`. EXPLICITLY EXCLUDED: `docs/primitives/README.md` and `docs/primitives/*.md` — both are PARTIALLY generated (README regenerates only the table between BEGIN/END PRIMITIVE CATALOG markers at lines 83/282; frontmatter script rewrites only YAML frontmatter, bodies are hand-authored prose).
design_calls.rationale: Keep-ours-then-regen is only correct when regen recreates the ENTIRE file from merged source. For a partially generated file, taking ours silently discards theirs-side hand-authored prose that regen will NOT restore — exactly the silent-wrong-pick class run-13 finding 1 exists to kill. README.md's 34 touches are overwhelmingly table churn, which regen repairs after a manual (now rare) conflict; the residual prose-conflict rate does not justify a silent-data-loss channel. The exclusion is pinned by the contract test, not prose.

design_calls.question: Where does the generated-file manifest live so .gitattributes cannot drift from reality?
design_calls.recommendation: One importable constant pair in `scripts/merge_generated.py`: `FULLY_GENERATED_PATTERNS` (the merge=generated set) and `PARTIALLY_GENERATED_EXCLUDED` (README + primitives docs, with rationale strings). The contract test parses .gitattributes and pins lockstep equality both ways; WS1's regen_all may import the same constants.
design_calls.rationale: One-definition seam per repo doctrine: the set exists today only implicitly across six regen scripts and three doc enumerations (the drift the plan's A1 documents). Putting it beside the driver keeps the driver, its installer, and its manifest in one file the test imports directly.

design_calls.question: Exact driver command: a repo script or bare `true`?
design_calls.recommendation: `merge.generated.driver = python scripts/merge_generated.py driver %O %A %B %P` — keeps %A (ours) untouched, prints ONE loud stderr line naming the path and the required follow-up (`run scripts/regen_all.py --write before committing this merge`), exits 0. If the behavioral test shows cwd-resolution of the relative script path is flaky cross-platform, fall back to `driver = true` (verified working) and move the notice into ensure-time docs — call this out to the integrator.
design_calls.rationale: Git runs merge drivers from the worktree top-level, so the relative `scripts/` path resolves in whichever worktree is merging; the fires-test proves the deployed command end-to-end rather than trusting this. The stderr notice closes the human gap between merge and regen; the hard gates behind it are the existing CI `--check` gates (ci.yml — regen-pr auto-heal on PRs, hard `--check` on main/claude/** pushes) plus the pre-commit regen hooks for clones that have them.

hazards.desc: Stale-generated window: an integrator commits a merge where the driver kept ours but forgets regen — HEAD then carries generated files inconsistent with merged source. CI --check fails loudly on push (main goes red), and a run-13 emergency wheel built from that HEAD would ship a stale operations.json/schemas.
hazards.mitigation: Driver prints the loud regen notice at merge time; the post-merge regen is already mandated integrator law; CI --check is the hard backstop so the state is never SILENT. LIVE-STATE flag: build any emergency wheel only from a CI-green HEAD (existing practice). Note the driver only changes LOCAL merge mechanics — nothing in the shipped wheel or any runtime path changes, so run 13 in flight is untouched.

hazards.desc: Manifest over-inclusion: if a hand-edited or partially generated file ever enters FULLY_GENERATED_PATTERNS, merges silently discard theirs-side content that regen does not restore.
hazards.mitigation: Contract test pins (a) .gitattributes merge=generated lines == FULLY_GENERATED_PATTERNS exactly, (b) PARTIALLY_GENERATED_EXCLUDED paths carry NO merge attribute, and (c) every manifest entry is an output path of one of the six regen scripts (string-pinned against the scripts' declared OUTPUT constants/docstrings). Builder must verify at build time that every src/hpc_agent/schemas/*.json is actually emitted by build_schemas.py --write (diff after a clean regen) before globbing the directory; any non-emitted file forces per-file listing instead of the glob.

hazards.desc: docs/internals/engineering-principles.md is contended: P1b (pack-seam workstream) adds an enforcement-map row to the same file; both units append table rows.
hazards.mitigation: My edit is one appended row + a 3-4 line subsection in a DIFFERENT section (repo-mechanics, not pack boundary); integrator merges table appends trivially. Flagged for merge ordering.

hazards.desc: Behavioral fires-test spawns real git subprocesses in tmp repos; user/global git config (autocrlf, init.defaultBranch, global attributes) can leak in and flake on CI runners vs this Windows box.
hazards.mitigation: Test isolates with GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL/GIT_CONFIG_SYSTEM pointed at empty files, `git init -b main`, explicit user.name/email, LF-only fixture content. Verified the whole scenario already runs green under Git Bash on Windows in the scratchpad experiment.

hazards.desc: Dependency: WS1's scripts/regen_all.py may land after this unit; without the ensure hook the driver never self-installs in fresh clones.
hazards.mitigation: Unit is standalone-correct without WS1: undeployed state degrades to today's conflict behavior (verified loudly-failing), and `python scripts/merge_generated.py ensure` is documented + runnable manually. Interface contract below tells WS1 exactly what to call; integrator wires the one line if WS1 merges first or later.

dive.test_plan: All in tests/contracts/test_generated_merge_driver.py, runnable via `.venv/Scripts/python.exe -m pytest tests/contracts/test_generated_merge_driver.py -q` (fast, no `slow` marker). (1) Lockstep pins: parse .gitattributes; the set of paths/globs carrying `merge=generated` equals scripts.merge_generated.FULLY_GENERATED_PATTERNS exactly (both directions); every PARTIALLY_GENERATED_EXCLUDED path matches NO merge attribute line; every manifest entry corresponds to a declared regen-script output (operations.json <- bake_operations_json.OUTPUT_PATH, _verb_module_map.py <- build_verb_module_map target, docs/generated/* <- build_operations_index.OUT, schemas glob <- build_schemas output dir). (2) The fires-AND-passes behavioral pair (the enforcement-map fire path): in an isolated tmp git repo (GIT_CONFIG_GLOBAL/SYSTEM neutralized, init -b main) with .gitattributes marking a probe file merge=generated and divergent edits on two branches — WITHOUT the driver configured, `git merge` exits nonzero with a conflict (the loud undeployed state fires); after `python scripts/merge_generated.py ensure`, the same merge exits 0 and the probe file equals ours byte-for-byte, and the driver's stderr notice names the path. (3) ensure idempotency: running ensure twice leaves `git config --get merge.generated.driver` identical; `check` exits 0 after ensure and nonzero in a virgin repo. (4) Existing suite must stay green: no source/generated files change, so no regen and no other suites are touched; ruff + mypy on the two new files pre-commit-style.
interface_contracts: REQUIRES (WS1, soft): scripts/regen_all.py calls `subprocess([sys.executable, 'scripts/merge_generated.py', 'ensure'])` (or imports scripts.merge_generated.ensure()) at the start of every invocation, so the driver self-installs in any clone that runs regen; unit is standalone-correct (loudly-degrading) until that line lands — integrator wires it whichever unit merges second.

PROVIDES: scripts/merge_generated.py with subcommands `driver %O %A %B %P` (keep-ours, stderr notice, exit 0), `ensure` (idempotent repo-local `git config merge.generated.driver/name` install), `check` (exit nonzero when missing/stale); importable constants FULLY_GENERATED_PATTERNS and PARTIALLY_GENERATED_EXCLUDED (the one definition of the fully-generated file set — WS1's regen_all and any future doctor check may import them).

PROVIDES: .gitattributes `merge=generated` lines for src/hpc_agent/operations.json, src/hpc_agent/schemas/*.json, src/hpc_agent/cli/_verb_module_map.py, docs/generated/* — pinned equal to the manifest by tests/contracts/test_generated_merge_driver.py.

PROVIDES: one new enforcement-map row in docs/internals/engineering-principles.md — 'the merge=generated attribute set equals the fully-generated manifest and never includes a partially generated file; the deployed driver keeps ours cleanly, the undeployed state conflicts loudly' — held by tests/contracts/test_generated_merge_driver.py with the demonstrated fire pair.

DOES NOT TOUCH: any generated file, any src/hpc_agent runtime path, .pre-commit-config.yaml, ci.yml, or the six regen scripts — zero collision with verb-adding units and zero behavior change for the in-flight run 13.
dive.summary: Kill the dominant swarm merge-conflict class (operations.json 48 touches, docs/generated/operations.md 36, docs/primitives/README.md 34, _verb_module_map.py 33 since 07-01) with a custom `merge=generated` keep-ours driver + mandatory post-merge regen, after honestly evaluating all three plan options. Key verified facts: git has no built-in attribute-level `ours` driver, so option (ii) needs per-clone config anyway; an attributes-declared but UNCONFIGURED driver degrades to a normal conflict (empirically verified — the undeployed state is loudly-failing, never silently wrong); worktrees share the clone's .git/config (verified via git-common-dir + config read from a scratch worktree), so one idempotent `ensure` per clone covers the whole agent-worktree swarm topology, and it self-deploys by riding WS1's regen_all (which the integrator must run after every merge wave anyway). Scope is restricted to 100%-regenerable files — docs/primitives/README.md and docs/primitives/*.md are explicitly excluded because they are partially hand-authored and keep-ours would silently drop theirs-side prose regen cannot restore. One new script (driver + ensure + check + the one-definition manifest), .gitattributes lines, one enforcement-map row, and a contract test with the fires-AND-passes pair (undeployed conflicts loudly / deployed keeps ours cleanly) plus manifest<->attributes lockstep pins. No regen, no generated files touched, no runtime behavior change — safe for the live run-13 constraint; the only new hazard (merge committed without regen) is already caught by CI's hard --check gates.

attacks.failure_mode: The behavioral fires-test as spec'd cannot pass: in the isolated tmp git repo there is no scripts/merge_generated.py, so the ensure-installed driver command `python scripts/merge_generated.py driver %O %A %B %P` (relative path, resolved from the merging worktree's top-level) fails at exec time, git reports 'merge driver failed' and leaves the file conflicted — the 'deployed merge exits 0 keeping ours' half of the pair goes red. The spec's test plan says 'after `python scripts/merge_generated.py ensure`, the same merge exits 0' without saying how the tmp repo can see the script.
attacks.evidence: Spec D5 pins the driver command to the relative path 'scripts/merge_generated.py' and test_plan item (2) runs ensure inside 'an isolated tmp git repo'. Repo scripts/ has no merge_generated.py yet (verified: ls scripts/ shows lint_*/build_* only), and the tmp repo git-init'd by the test will contain only the probe file + .gitattributes. Compare tests/infra/test_audit_fixes.py:212 which had to solve the identical problem by copying 'the script into the fake repo's scripts/ and run it there' — precedent that repo-relative scripts don't resolve from fixture repos.
attacks.mitigation: Spec the test explicitly: either copy merge_generated.py into <tmp>/scripts/ before ensure (the test_audit_fixes pattern), or give ensure a --driver-path override the test uses to write an absolute sys.executable + script path into the tmp repo's config. Keep the production ensure writing the relative path.

attacks.failure_mode: If the implementing agent resolves the tmp-repo problem (or the D5 'flaky cross-platform' fallback) by writing an ABSOLUTE path into merge.generated.driver, it breaks on this exact box: the repo lives at 'C:\Users\james\CC Allowed\hpc-agent' — the path contains a SPACE, and git executes merge drivers via sh -c, so an unquoted absolute path splits at 'CC' and the driver fails on every merge in the real repo (loud conflict, but the feature is dead where it matters most: the swarm clone).
attacks.evidence: env working directory = C:\Users\james\CC Allowed\hpc-agent (space in 'CC Allowed'). Spec D5's fallback discussion ('if cwd-resolution of the relative script path is flaky... fall back to driver = true') never mentions quoting or the space; an autonomous agent choosing sys.executable-absolute-path (the obvious 'robust' fix) hits it. sys.executable here is also under a space-free venv path but the script path is not.
attacks.mitigation: Add to D5: any absolute path written into git config MUST be sh-quoted (and the fires-test must run ensure+merge inside a directory whose path contains a space, mirroring this clone). Prefer the relative-path production config + test-only override so the shipped config never embeds an absolute path.

attacks.failure_mode: Contract test parses 'the repo's .gitattributes' — but there are TWO: the root one and packs/quant/.gitattributes ('* -text', byte-pinning for sealed pack files). An agent globbing **/.gitattributes or walking the tree pins the union and the lockstep test fails immediately (or worse, the agent adds merge lines to the pack's file, breaking the pack seal semantics).
attacks.evidence: packs/quant/README.md:24 documents packs/quant/.gitattributes with '* -text'; root .gitattributes (read in full) has only text/eol/binary lines today. Spec D4/test_plan say only 'parse .gitattributes' with no root-only qualifier.
attacks.mitigation: Pin the test to REPO_ROOT/'.gitattributes' by absolute path and add one assertion that no OTHER .gitattributes in the tree carries merge=generated.

attacks.failure_mode: New scripts/merge_generated.py trips repo lints the spec never mentions: lint_text_io_encoding scans scripts/*.py and rejects any text-mode open/read_text/write_text/subprocess(text=True) without encoding= (the driver must read/write %A and the config), and CI runs `ruff check .` over scripts/ too. An agent writing plain open(a_path) gets a red 3.12 lint leg after an otherwise green implementation.
attacks.evidence: .pre-commit-config.yaml:26-29 — lint_text_io_encoding files regex '^(src/hpc_agent/.*\.py|scripts/[^/]+\.py)$'; scripts/lint_text_io_encoding.py:32-34 SCAN_GLOBS includes ('scripts', '*.py'); ci.yml:133 'ruff check .'.
attacks.mitigation: One line in the spec's test_plan/coding notes: all text I/O in merge_generated.py uses encoding='utf-8' (or reads %A/%B in binary mode, which also sidesteps eol questions since keep-ours should be byte-preserving anyway — binary copy is strictly safer).

attacks.failure_mode: Rebase/cherry-pick ours-inversion: git swaps ours/theirs during rebase, so an agent worktree rebasing its branch onto main has 'ours' = main — the keep-ours driver silently discards the branch's OWN generated-file changes at every rebase step. Self-healing only if regen runs afterward; an agent that rebases, runs the (green — source is intact) suite, and pushes gets a red --check on claude/** or relies on the PR auto-heal. Not silent-wrong at HEAD-of-main, but a recurring agent-confusion tax the spec never mentions, and the stderr notice fires mid-rebase where agents rarely look.
attacks.evidence: Git documented behavior (merge driver invoked by rebase's merge machinery with reversed sides); ci.yml:32-37 + 117-123 confirm the PR auto-heal and the hard --check on main/claude/** pushes, so the end state is loud, not silent. Spec D1/D2/hazards discuss only 'merge' semantics.
attacks.mitigation: Document the inversion in the engineering-principles row and the ensure-time notice ('after any merge OR rebase touching generated files, run regen'); no code change needed since regen restores from merged source either way.

attacks.failure_mode: Disjoint-ownership violation as declared: docs/internals/engineering-principles.md appears in files_owned while the spec's own hazard 3 admits the P1b pack-seam workstream edits the same file in the same wave. Two Opus agents with 'ownership' of one file is exactly the conflict class an orchestrator enforcing disjointness will either reject or let collide.
attacks.evidence: Spec files_owned includes 'docs/internals/engineering-principles.md'; spec hazard 3 verbatim: 'P1b (pack-seam workstream) adds an enforcement-map row to the same file; both units append table rows.' Both cannot own it under the stated swarm model.
attacks.mitigation: Demote the file to a declared shared-append target resolved by the integrator (or have WS2 emit its row as a patch snippet the integrator applies), and say so in interface_contracts instead of files_owned.

attacks.failure_mode: The driver replaces git's text merge for EVERY both-sides-changed generated file — including merges that would have auto-merged cleanly — so post-merge regen becomes mandatory on essentially every integration merge, and WS1's scripts/regen_all.py DOES NOT EXIST yet (verified). Until WS1 lands, 'run regen' means running six scripts by hand in the right order; the driver's stderr notice pointing at 'scripts/regen_all.py --write' would name a nonexistent file if WS2 merges first.
attacks.evidence: ls scripts/regen_all.py → 'No such file or directory'; spec's files_read_only lists scripts/regen_all.py as if readable, and D5's notice text hardcodes 'run scripts/regen_all.py --write'. The six-script regen list is real (memory: dev_regen_list — missing one cost 4 test failures in 0.8.1) and .pre-commit-config.yaml:58-99 shows the six regen entries.
attacks.mitigation: Make the notice text a constant that names regen_all when present else lists the six scripts (or just names both: 'scripts/regen_all.py --write (or the six build_*/bake_* scripts)'); remove regen_all.py from files_read_only and mark it PLANNED-BY-WS1 so the implementing agent doesn't stall trying to read it.

attacks.failure_mode: Stale-generated HEAD on main during run-13: integrator merges a wave (driver keeps ours), forgets regen, pushes — main goes red on the --check gates. Spec correctly calls this out; residual risk is only the emergency-wheel-from-non-green-HEAD case, which existing practice (build only from CI-green HEAD) covers. Included to confirm the spec's own analysis holds: verified the hard gates exist exactly as cited.
attacks.evidence: ci.yml:199 'python scripts/build_schemas.py --check', :202 'python scripts/bake_operations_json.py --check', plus frontmatter --check at :142, all on push to main/claude/**; the PR job (:32-37) auto-heals instead. .gitattributes/merge driver ships nothing into the wheel, so run-13 runtime is genuinely untouched.
attacks.mitigation: None beyond the spec's own (already adequate): CI --check is the backstop; keep the LIVE-STATE 'wheel only from green HEAD' line in the integrator notes.

missing_from_dive: The tmp-repo behavioral test cannot exec a repo-relative driver path — the exact fixture-copy problem tests/infra/test_audit_fixes.py:212 already solved in this repo; the spec's test plan is unimplementable as written without the copy/override.
The repo path contains a space ('CC Allowed') — any absolute-path driver command needs sh-quoting; never mentioned despite D5 explicitly contemplating a path-resolution fallback.
Rebase/cherry-pick invert ours/theirs, so agent-branch rebases silently drop the branch's own generated changes until regen — the notice/docs should say 'merge OR rebase'.
scripts/*.py falls under lint_text_io_encoding (pre-commit-config:29, SCAN_GLOBS in the lint) and CI's repo-wide ruff — the new script has lint obligations the spec never lists.
A second .gitattributes exists (packs/quant/.gitattributes, '* -text') — the lockstep test must pin root-only and assert no other attributes file grows merge=generated.
scripts/regen_all.py is listed in files_read_only but does not exist (WS1 hasn't landed) — the D5 stderr notice hardcodes its name; needs a graceful fallback naming the six real scripts.

Positive note the dive earned but didn't state: the workstream brief's path 'src/hpc_agent/config/operations.json' is wrong — the file is src/hpc_agent/operations.json (bake_operations_json.py:41 OUTPUT_PATH = PACKAGE_ROOT/'operations.json'); the spec silently used the correct path, which should be flagged to the orchestrator so other workstreams don't inherit the brief's stale path.

CI is ubuntu-only (ci.yml runs-on: ubuntu-latest everywhere), so the Windows-side driver behavior the dive verified locally will never be covered by CI — acceptable, but worth stating as a standing gap since the swarm clone is Windows.

design_calls.question: True dependency order of the six regen scripts + the pending-docs check?
design_calls.recommendation: build_schemas.py -> bake_operations_json.py -> build_primitive_frontmatter.py -> build_primitive_index.py -> build_operations_index.py -> build_verb_module_map.py -> check_no_pending_primitive_docs.py
design_calls.rationale: operations_catalog resolves input/output schema names by FILE EXISTENCE in src/hpc_agent/schemas/ (operations.py:142-150), so schemas must exist before bake_operations_json (registry projection) and build_operations_index (subprocess `capabilities`). build_primitive_index parses per-doc frontmatter (primitives_from_registry reads md_path; parse_frontmatter raises on missing required fields) and a new primitive's doc only exists after build_primitive_frontmatter --write scaffolds the stub — frontmatter strictly precedes index. build_verb_module_map is registry-only and order-free; placed sixth for determinism. check_no_pending runs last so a freshly scaffolded stub fails loudly (correct: human must fill the body).

design_calls.question: Invoke the six scripts as subprocesses or import their main()s?
design_calls.recommendation: Subprocesses: [sys.executable, scripts/<name>.py, <mode-args>], cwd=REPO_ROOT, no shell.
design_calls.rationale: Each script mutates process state that must precede the FIRST hpc_agent import (os.environ.setdefault('HPC_AGENT_DISABLE_PLUGINS','1'), sys.path inserts) and the primitive registry caches register_primitives(); in-process chaining risks env-timing and cache-order bugs (#198 class). build_operations_index already subprocess-calls the CLI. Subprocess isolation exactly matches how pre-commit/CI invoke them today, so behavior is provably unchanged. ~20s serial is the accepted cost (plan baseline).

design_calls.question: How does --check/--write map per script, and what does bare `regen_all` do?
design_calls.recommendation: regen_all accepts exactly --check or --write and REFUSES bare invocation with usage. Per-script argv map: --check -> ['--check'] for all six + [] for check_no_pending (always a check). --write -> ['--write'] for build_schemas/bake_operations_json/build_primitive_frontmatter/build_verb_module_map; [] (bare = writes) for build_primitive_index/build_operations_index; check_no_pending still runs last.
design_calls.rationale: The scripts' bare-invocation semantics are inconsistent (schemas/bake/frontmatter: diff-preview; index scripts: write; verb_module_map: check-like rc 1 WITHOUT writing) — a single entry point must not inherit that ambiguity. Passing --write to the index scripts would silently no-op-parse (they only test '--check' in sys.argv), so the map uses bare argv there; verb_module_map REQUIRES explicit --write or nothing regenerates (the exact trap that makes the current recipe ship a stale map).

design_calls.question: Fail-fast or run-all on step failure?
design_calls.recommendation: Run every step in order regardless of failures, print one PASS/FAIL line per step, exit nonzero if any failed (both modes).
design_calls.rationale: In --check mode one run should surface ALL drift (matches CI ergonomics where gates are separate steps); in --write mode later steps consume earlier outputs but a failed earlier step just makes later diffs loud, never silently wrong — and full visibility beats a truncated report for a 7-step ~20s run.

design_calls.question: scripts/regen_all.py or a console-script in pyproject?
design_calls.recommendation: Plain scripts/regen_all.py, no pyproject change.
design_calls.rationale: Console-script means touching pyproject (packaging surface) while run 13 is live against wheel e2ff358a and an emergency wheel may be cut from HEAD — zero-risk wins. CI/pre-commit/docs already invoke `python scripts/...`; scripts/ is not packaged, so the wheel is byte-unaffected.

design_calls.question: What is the ledger-parsing test's table contract so the format can't silently break the parser?
design_calls.recommendation: tests/contracts/test_regen_debt_ledger.py with pure helpers parse_ledger(text)/check_rows(rows) operating on a string. Contract: (1) heading '## Outstanding regen debt' must exist and be followed by a pipe table with the EXACT 5-column header 'Item | Source drift log | What is owed | Live gate today | Owner / wave' — any deviation (missing heading, wrong header, wrong cell count) is a hard FAIL, so a format change disables nothing silently; (2) every row's 'Live gate today' cell must contain >=1 backticked pytest reference (regex `test_\w+` or `tests/\S+\.py`) or the literal 'no live gate'; each referenced test_* name must exist somewhere under tests/ (source grep), so a renamed/deleted gate fails loudly; (3) rows whose cell contains '**RED**' are the strict-xfail punch list: the test runs the named gate via `[sys.executable, -m, pytest, -q, <target>]` — gate still failing -> pytest.xfail (debt outstanding, suite stays green); gate now PASSING -> hard FAIL 'debt paid — remove the row' (the strict-xpass semantics of the test_recovery_registry precedent); (4) zero rows -> pass (headers still asserted). Fires-AND-passes pair: unit tests feed synthetic ledger text (RED-claimed-but-green row -> fail; malformed header -> fail; outstanding RED row -> xfail; clean table -> pass) to the pure helpers — no pytest-in-pytest in the steady state, since after reconciliation zero RED rows remain.
design_calls.rationale: The 'Live gate today' column is prose; anchoring on backticked test names + the **RED** token (both already the ledger's de-facto convention) mechanizes it without rewriting rows, and the strict header check is the anti-silent-break guard the workstream brief demands. Subprocess pytest only runs for RED rows, which this unit reduces to zero.

design_calls.question: The ledger's 6 outstanding rows are stale on HEAD (notebook-draft IS in _SPEC_VERBS, evidence_brief roundtrip passes, all regen --check gates green on main) — land the gate red or reconcile first?
design_calls.recommendation: Reconcile in the SAME commit-series: for each row, run its named live gate + regen_all --check on HEAD; every row verified green moves to the 'Checked — no outstanding debt' section (or is deleted) and the originating drift-log note in docs/design/{registration-kernel,data-trace,challenge-attestation,multi-human}.md collapses to the one-line 'paid — see the ledger' pointer the ledger's own pay-down procedure (steps 1-3) specifies. Also fix regen-debt-ledger.md:20-22 to name the now-real test and correct its stale precedent path (actual: tests/contracts/test_recovery_registry.py, not tests/ops/recover/).
design_calls.rationale: LIVE-STATE: a strict gate landed against a stale ledger is a permanently red test on HEAD — forbidden. The verification is mechanical (run the named gates); CI green on e2ff358a is strong prior evidence all six debts were paid by intervening serial regens.

design_calls.question: How far into ci.yml/docs.yml does re-pointing go?
design_calls.recommendation: ONLY replace the 3.12 PR-leg local-regen step (ci.yml:124-130) with `python scripts/regen_all.py --write` (this also fixes its missing build_verb_module_map regen). Do NOT touch the regen-pr job (ci.yml:61-84) or docs.yml: their auto-commit file_patterns are deliberately disjoint (non-docs vs docs/) to prevent push races — pointing either at regen-all would regenerate across the ownership boundary. Leave the individual --check gates (ci.yml:140-145,197-202; docs.yml:115-124) as-is.
design_calls.rationale: The disjoint auto-commit split is a documented race fix (ci.yml:62-67); consolidating it is out of A1's scope and risky. The local-regen step writes without committing, so regen-all is safe and strictly better there. Keeping the --check gates granular preserves per-gate CI diagnostics and avoids colliding with the A3/A4 CI unit editing the same file.

design_calls.question: Pre-commit re-point shape?
design_calls.recommendation: Replace the seven hooks build-primitive-frontmatter / build-primitive-index / build-verb-module-map / build-operations-index / build-schemas / bake-operations-json / check-no-pending-primitive-docs with ONE `regen-all` hook: `entry: python scripts/regen_all.py --write`, `pass_filenames: false`, `files: '^(src/hpc_agent/.*\.py|docs/primitives/.*\.md)$'` (the union of the seven triggers; the schemas/*.json trigger is subsumed by src/hpc_agent/). Keep lint-primitive-doc-templates and all other lint hooks untouched.
design_calls.rationale: One hook = one recipe = the whole point of A1; the union trigger means any src .py change runs all seven steps (~20s) instead of a subset — accepted cost per the plan, and idempotent no-ops keep it quiet. The header comment 'the four regen scripts' (pre-commit:4-8) gets corrected in the same edit.

design_calls.question: adding-a-primitive.md fix shape (A7)?
design_calls.recommendation: Line 4: drop the '167' literal for verify-live phrasing ('the patterns the existing primitives follow — `hpc-agent capabilities` is the live count'). Step 2 and step 7: replace the enumerations with `uv run python scripts/regen_all.py --write` + `uv run python scripts/lint_primitive_doc_templates.py`, stating regen-all runs all SIX generators in dependency order plus the pending-docs check; 'Pre-commit runs all five' becomes 'Pre-commit runs regen-all and the doc-template lint'. Add one sentence noting a new ungrouped verb also regenerates cli/_verb_module_map.py (the previously omitted step). sync-checklist.md:197-204 and HANDOFF.md:62-67: collapse each enumeration to a regen-all pointer (HANDOFF gets a minimal '(now `scripts/regen_all.py`)' touch — it is a banked historical doc).
design_calls.rationale: Deleting the per-doc enumerations is what A1 mandates ('delete the per-doc enumerations'); count literals are A6's lint territory, so A7 just removes the rot vector rather than updating 167->169 (which re-rots at 170).

design_calls.question: Enforcement-map row for the new gate?
design_calls.recommendation: Add ONE row to the relevant enforcement-map section of docs/internals/engineering-principles.md: 'a regen-debt-ledger row's status claim matches its live gate — held by tests/contracts/test_regen_debt_ledger.py (fires: synthetic RED-claimed-but-green row)'. Append-only, at the section end.
design_calls.rationale: Workstream brief: every mechanizable rule needs a row with a demonstrated fire path. Append-at-end minimizes merge conflict with other units (P1b also adds a row); integrator resolves ordering.

hazards.desc: Shared-surface collisions: .github/workflows/ci.yml is also edited by the unit owning A3/A4 (slow-tier gating at ci.yml:205-234, coverage re-run); docs/internals/engineering-principles.md gains rows from the P1b unit; .pre-commit-config.yaml could be touched by any lint-adding unit.
hazards.mitigation: My ci.yml edit is confined to the single step at lines 124-130; my engineering-principles edit is one appended row; integrator sequences these files and resolves trivial adjacency conflicts. Declared here so disjointness computation flags them.

hazards.desc: Landing the strict ledger gate with any genuinely-unpaid row makes HEAD's suite red (violates the live-state constraint).
hazards.mitigation: Ledger reconciliation is in the same unit and the same commit-series as the test (DC7); builder runs every named gate + regen_all --check on HEAD before removing a row; a row that is NOT verifiably paid stays and is marked **RED** so the gate xfails it instead of failing.

hazards.desc: Drift-log 'paid' pointer edits touch four heavily-owned design docs (registration-kernel.md, data-trace.md, challenge-attestation.md, multi-human.md) that pack-seam units may also edit.
hazards.mitigation: Edits are one-line replacements inside each doc's drift-log regen-debt note only, per the ledger's own pay-down procedure step 3; if the integrator prefers, these four edits can be split into a trailing follow-up commit that only this unit produces.

hazards.desc: Pre-commit consolidation runs all seven steps (~20s serial, imports the full registry 6x) on every src .py commit where today a subset ran.
hazards.mitigation: Accepted by plan A1; steps are idempotent no-ops when clean. If it proves painful, per-step file filters can be reintroduced inside regen_all later without changing the recipe surface.

hazards.desc: regen_all's per-script argv map hard-codes each script's flag semantics (index scripts have no --write; verb_module_map's bare default is check-like); a future flag change in a script silently desyncs the map.
hazards.mitigation: regen_all asserts each step's rc and, in --check mode, treats ANY nonzero rc as drift naming the step; a smoke test in tests/ (part of the fires/passes pair work) runs regen_all --check against the clean tree so a flag desync surfaces as a CI failure, not silence.

hazards.desc: Behavior change for in-flight run 13: none — scripts/ and docs/ are not packaged, operations.json/schemas are byte-unchanged, no runtime module is touched; the wheel from HEAD stays identical in behavior.
hazards.mitigation: No mitigation needed; stated for the integrator's live-state audit. The only new CI-visible artifact is one test file and one script.

dive.test_plan: 1) tests/contracts/test_regen_debt_ledger.py: fires-AND-passes pairs on synthetic ledger text via the pure parse_ledger/check_rows helpers — (a) RED-claimed row whose gate passes -> hard fail; (b) malformed header/heading/cell-count -> hard fail; (c) outstanding RED row with failing gate -> xfail; (d) clean table + green gates -> pass; plus the live test against docs/internals/regen-debt-ledger.md, which must be GREEN on HEAD after reconciliation (every named test_* reference resolves under tests/). 2) Pre-removal verification: run each ledger row's named gate (tests/contracts/test_primitive_remediation.py::test_spec_verb_inventory_matches_cli, tests/_wire/test_schema_models_roundtrip.py -k evidence_brief, the doc-template lint test, the roundtrip suite) — already spot-verified green on HEAD. 3) regen_all: `python scripts/regen_all.py --check` exits 0 on the clean tree; `--write` twice is idempotent (second run reports all steps clean, `git status` empty); mutate one generated file (e.g. touch a byte in cli/_verb_module_map.py) -> `--check` exits nonzero naming exactly that step; bare invocation exits nonzero with usage. 4) `pre-commit run -a` green with the consolidated hook. 5) Targeted pytest: tests/contracts/test_regen_debt_ledger.py + tests/scripts/ green locally; full ~9,800 suite at CI per the CI-on-GitHub directive.
interface_contracts: PROVIDES scripts/regen_all.py: modes exactly `--check` | `--write` (bare invocation refuses with usage, rc!=0); runs, as subprocesses of sys.executable with cwd=repo root, in fixed order: build_schemas -> bake_operations_json -> build_primitive_frontmatter -> build_primitive_index -> build_operations_index -> build_verb_module_map -> check_no_pending_primitive_docs; per-step PASS/FAIL lines; exits 0 iff every step exited 0. Other units (A2 post-merge driver, future waves) invoke it as `python scripts/regen_all.py --write` / `--check` — this invocation string is the stable seam.

PROVIDES the ledger table contract (enforced by tests/contracts/test_regen_debt_ledger.py): the '## Outstanding regen debt' table keeps the exact 5-column header 'Item | Source drift log | What is owed | Live gate today | Owner / wave'; every row's Live-gate cell carries >=1 backticked `test_*`/`tests/*.py` reference (or literally 'no live gate'); '**RED**' in that cell marks a strict-xfail punch-list row. Any wave adding a regen-debt row must follow this format or the gate fails.
PROVIDES one appended enforcement-map row in docs/internals/engineering-principles.md naming tests/contracts/test_regen_debt_ledger.py as the holder.

REQUIRES: the integrator owns all generated files (operations.json, src/hpc_agent/schemas/, docs/generated/, docs/primitives/README.md + frontmatter, cli/_verb_module_map.py) — this unit never commits regen output (regen.needed=false: no @primitive/model/schema change).
REQUIRES from the A3/A4 CI unit: no edits to ci.yml lines 115-130 (the PR local-regen step this unit rewrites); this unit does not touch ci.yml:203+ (slow tier / coverage).
DOES NOT touch: docs.yml, the ci.yml regen-pr job (61-84) — the docs-vs-nondocs auto-commit disjointness stands.
dive.summary: WS1-regen-story (plan A1+A5+A7): (a) new scripts/regen_all.py — a subprocess-driven single entry point running the six regen scripts in the verified dependency order build_schemas -> bake_operations_json -> build_primitive_frontmatter -> build_primitive_index -> build_operations_index -> build_verb_module_map, then check_no_pending_primitive_docs, with a per-script argv map absorbing the scripts' inconsistent flag semantics (index scripts have no --write; verb_module_map's bare default checks without writing) and exactly two modes (--check/--write, bare refuses); pre-commit's seven regen hooks collapse to one, ci.yml's 3.12 PR local-regen step re-points at it (fixing its missing verb-map regen), and the three divergent doc enumerations (adding-a-primitive.md step 7, sync-checklist.md 197-204, HANDOFF.md 62-67) become pointers. (b) the regen-debt-ledger strict-xfail gate tests/contracts/test_regen_debt_ledger.py: strict 5-column table parse (format deviation = hard fail), named-gate existence check, **RED** rows executed via subprocess pytest with strict-xpass semantics ('debt paid — remove the row'), fires/passes pairs on synthetic text; since all six current ledger rows are verifiably paid on HEAD (spot-verified: notebook-draft in _SPEC_VERBS, evidence_brief roundtrip green, CI green), the same commit-series reconciles the ledger and collapses the four originating drift-log notes so the gate lands green — plus one appended enforcement-map row. (c) adding-a-primitive.md content fixes: 167->verify-live phrasing (actual count 169), 'all five'->the regen-all pointer, the omitted build_verb_module_map step covered. No runtime module, no packaged file, and no generated artifact changes — HEAD stays wheel-identical for the in-flight run 13. Plan-doc drift found: the strict-xfail precedent lives at tests/contracts/test_recovery_registry.py, not tests/ops/recover/.

attacks.failure_mode: The hazard-5 'smoke test in tests/ runs regen_all --check against the clean tree' breaks CI's self-heal architecture: as a pytest test it executes on legs that never run the local regen — the 3.10/3.11 matrix legs (the local --write step is gated `matrix.python-version == '3.12' && pull_request`, ci.yml:124), the test-windows lane, and every push event. Worse, its verb-map step has NO auto-heal path anywhere: the regen-pr job regenerates only schemas+operations.json (ci.yml:68-70) and its auto-commit file_pattern is 'src/hpc_agent/operations.json src/hpc_agent/schemas/' (ci.yml:82); docs.yml's pattern is 'docs/primitives/ docs/generated/operations.md' (docs.yml:107). cli/_verb_module_map.py — a PACKAGED runtime module — is committed back by nobody, and today NO CI gate checks it at all (verified absent from both workflows). A PR whose author skipped pre-commit after adding a verb currently merges silently; with the smoke test, that PR goes red on exactly the 3.10/3.11/windows legs (green on 3.12, which healed locally) with no bot fix, and a direct push to a claude/** branch (or the ledger test itself if it subprocesses regen_all) reproduces the red without any regen step ever running.
attacks.evidence: ci.yml:13-16 ('every gate self-heals regardless of which workflow lands the committed drift'), ci.yml:115-130 (local regen 3.12+PR only, and it currently omits build_verb_module_map), ci.yml:82 and docs.yml:107 (disjoint file_patterns, neither covers src/hpc_agent/cli/_verb_module_map.py), ci.yml:131-202 + docs.yml:111-124 (no verb-map --check gate exists anywhere today). Verified all six --check gates pass on this HEAD, so the test lands green — the trap fires on the first post-merge verb-map skew.
attacks.mitigation: Don't put the clean-tree regen_all --check assertion in the pytest suite. Make it a 3.12-leg CI STEP placed after the local-regen step (mirroring every existing --check gate), or gate the pytest version behind an env var set only on that leg. Separately, either add build_verb_module_map.py --write to the regen-pr job and 'src/hpc_agent/cli/_verb_module_map.py' to its file_pattern (consistent with that job's non-docs ownership; relaxes DC8's 'don't touch 61-84' by one line each) or accept that verb-map drift stays pre-commit-only and exclude it from any strict CI assertion.

attacks.failure_mode: DC6's ledger-table contract is built on a false premise: it claims backticked test refs + **RED** are 'already the ledger's de-facto convention', but 3 of the 7 current rows have prose-only 'Live gate today' cells with neither a backticked test nor the literal 'no live gate' — row 2 'Present-only key; readers tolerant.', row 3 'Byte-identical for untraced pairs (pinned by test).', row 5 'Registry arithmetic relative; regen deferred.'. The contract only lands green because DC7 empties the table. If reconciliation stalls on any one of those rows (the builder can't verify it paid), the spec's fallback — 'stays and is marked **RED**' — is unimplementable: **RED** rows require a runnable named gate for the subprocess-pytest xfail, and these rows have none. The agent is left with a row the parser hard-fails and no compliant way to express it.
attacks.evidence: docs/internals/regen-debt-ledger.md:29,30,32 (prose-only Live-gate cells) vs DC6 clause (2). Likelihood is low only because I re-verified the reconciliation premise: all six regen --check gates pass on HEAD, test_spec_verb_inventory_matches_cli passes, evidence_brief roundtrip passes (9 passed; the 1 xfail in the -k selection is an unrelated remediation-prose test).
attacks.mitigation: Add one sentence to DC7: any row that survives reconciliation must be REWRITTEN into the contract format (a backticked runnable gate, or 'no live gate' — and define whether 'no live gate' rows may carry **RED**, which should be a hard format error since there is nothing to xfail).

attacks.failure_mode: Undeclared new file breaks swarm disjointness: hazard 5's mitigation and the test plan ('tests/scripts/ green locally') require a regen_all smoke-test file, but new_files lists only scripts/regen_all.py and tests/contracts/test_regen_debt_ledger.py. An autonomous agent will create tests/scripts/test_regen_all.py (or similar) that the integrator's ownership computation never saw.
attacks.evidence: Spec new_files array vs hazard 5 mitigation text ('a smoke test in tests/ ... runs regen_all --check') and test_plan item 5 ('tests/contracts/test_regen_debt_ledger.py + tests/scripts/ green locally'). tests/scripts/ exists and holds other units' lint tests (e.g. tests/scripts/test_lint_library_knowledge.py).
attacks.mitigation: Add tests/scripts/test_regen_all.py to new_files explicitly (and, per attack 1, decide whether the clean-tree assertion lives there or as a CI step before writing it).

attacks.failure_mode: DC9's union trigger regex silently drops a today-live trigger: '^(src/hpc_agent/.*\.py|docs/primitives/.*\.md)$' does not match src/hpc_agent/schemas/*.json, yet the current build-operations-index hook fires on '^(src/hpc_agent/.*\.py|src/hpc_agent/schemas/.*\.json)$'. The spec's claim 'the schemas/*.json trigger is subsumed by src/hpc_agent/' is wrong (the alternative requires .py). A commit touching only a schema JSON (hand-revert, merge-conflict resolution) no longer runs regen locally; only CI's --check catches it.
attacks.evidence: .pre-commit-config.yaml:86 (operations-index files pattern includes src/hpc_agent/schemas/.*\.json) vs DC9's proposed pattern text.
attacks.mitigation: Use '^(src/hpc_agent/.*\.(py|json)|docs/primitives/.*\.md)$' or add the schemas json alternation verbatim.

attacks.failure_mode: DC3's load-bearing rationale is factually wrong: passing --write to build_primitive_index/build_operations_index would NOT 'silently no-op-parse' — both compute check = '--check' in sys.argv and, when false, fall through to the WRITE path (so --write writes, same as bare). The chosen bare-argv map still behaves correctly, but an agent that trusts the stated semantics may build the flag-desync smoke assertions or refusal logic around a false model of the scripts (e.g. asserting the index scripts reject --write).
attacks.evidence: scripts/build_primitive_index.py:174-190 and scripts/build_operations_index.py:154-171 (single --check flag; absence of --check reaches the write branch). Also verified DC3's other claims are correct: build_verb_module_map bare-with-drift returns rc 1 WITHOUT writing (scripts/build_verb_module_map.py:104-117), schemas/bake/frontmatter bare = diff preview.
attacks.mitigation: Correct the rationale: bare and --write are equivalent for the index scripts; the map may pass either. Keep the smoke test's oracle as 'regen_all --check exits 0 on a clean tree / nonzero naming the mutated step', which is semantics-independent.

attacks.failure_mode: DC6's gate-existence check ('each referenced test_* name must exist somewhere under tests/ (source grep)') is ambiguous for file-stem references: the current ledger row 7 cites `test_lint_primitive_doc_templates`, which is a FILE (tests/contracts/test_lint_primitive_doc_templates.py), not a function defined anywhere — a content-grep for 'def test_lint_primitive_doc_templates' fails. Moot for the current rows (all removed by DC7) but the contract binds future waves; an agent implementing content-grep-only produces a parser that rejects legitimate file-stem gate references.
attacks.evidence: docs/internals/regen-debt-ledger.md:34 names test_spec_verb_inventory_matches_cli (a function, tests/contracts/test_primitive_remediation.py:491) AND test_lint_primitive_doc_templates (a filename only — tests/contracts/test_lint_primitive_doc_templates.py exists; no such function).
attacks.mitigation: Define existence as: function-name grep over tests/ content OR filename-stem match over tests/ paths. One sentence in DC6.

attacks.failure_mode: Cross-unit coordination gap on docs/internals/adding-a-primitive.md: the plan's A6 unit ('frozen-count literal lint', devx plan line 136-140) explicitly targets the SAME file's line-4 '167 primitives' literal for its lint/allowlist work while WS1 owns the file and rewrites that exact line (DC10). If the A6 unit adds an allowlist entry or asserts on the literal WS1 just deleted, one of the two lands broken. Note also the literal is currently GREEN under the existing pin (test_prose_primitive_count tolerance ±2; 169-167=2), so 'content drift' is at the tolerance edge, not a red gate — removal is right, but the A6 unit must know the literal is gone. Minor same-file drift: the enumeration/'all five' sentence actually sits at lines 233-243, not 236-241.
attacks.evidence: docs/plans/devx-and-pack-seams-2026-07-15.md:136-140 (A6 cites adding-a-primitive.md:4); tests/contracts/test_prose_primitive_count.py:61 (_TOLERANCE=2) and :37 (file scanned); grep of adding-a-primitive.md: '167' at line 4, 'Pre-commit runs all five' at line 243.
attacks.mitigation: Add an interface_contracts line: 'PROVIDES to the A6 unit: adding-a-primitive.md carries NO numeric primitive-count literal after this unit; A6's lint/allowlist must not reference one.' Integrator sequences WS1 before A6.

missing_from_dive: cli/_verb_module_map.py has NO --check gate anywhere in CI today (neither ci.yml nor docs.yml), and NO workflow's auto-commit file_pattern covers it (ci.yml:82, docs.yml:107) — the spec 'fixes the missing verb-map regen' in the local step but never notices that its new clean-tree assertion becomes the map's FIRST-EVER gate with zero self-heal path, on a packaged runtime module.

The ci.yml self-heal doctrine (header comment, lines 3-16: every --check gate is preceded by a local --write on the leg that runs it) — a pytest-embedded regen check violates it on the 3.10/3.11 legs, the test-windows lane, and all push events; the dive never addresses where in the matrix its smoke test executes.

Three of the seven current ledger rows have prose-only 'Live gate today' cells (regen-debt-ledger.md:29,30,32), directly contradicting DC6's 'already the de-facto convention' premise — the contract works only because DC7 empties the table first.
The smoke-test file for regen_all is required by hazard 5 and the test plan but absent from new_files — the swarm disjointness computation misses it.
A6 (frozen-count lint unit) targets the same adding-a-primitive.md:4 literal WS1 deletes — no coordination contract declared for that unit (only A3/A4 and P1b are named).

Positive verifications the dive asserted but didn't show: I confirmed on HEAD that all six regen --check gates + check_no_pending pass (including build_verb_module_map, on Windows), that test_spec_verb_inventory_matches_cli and the evidence_brief roundtrip pass, that the strict-xfail precedent really lives at tests/contracts/test_recovery_registry.py (ledger:19's tests/ops/recover/ path is indeed stale), that operations.py:142-152 resolves schemas by file existence, and that the ledger's 'Checked — no outstanding debt' section (line 36) and pay-down steps 1-3 (lines 46-55) exist as DC7 describes — the reconciliation premise is solid.

cross_unit_conflicts.resource: src/hpc_agent/state/pack_declarations.py + tests/state/test_pack_declarations.py (HARD file overlap, declared unavoidable)
cross_unit_conflicts.resolution: Strict serialization: WS6 merges first, WS7 builds on WS6's merged code. Function-level disjointness (WS6 owns compose_* at :655-727 and the compose test block :355-416; WS7 owns the resolve_* echoes and generalizes fixture helpers :51-104) is NOT enough to parallelize safely — WS7 rewrites the shared fixture helpers (_build_pack/_opted_in) that WS6's new tests will call, so parallel branches guarantee a semantic (not just textual) merge conflict. Interface-first parallelism is NOT sound here.

cross_unit_conflicts.resource: .github/workflows/ci.yml (HARD overlap — both list it in files_owned; WS3 claims exclusive ownership)
cross_unit_conflicts.resolution: Route WS1's ci.yml hunk (re-pointing the PR local-regen step at ~:115-130 to `python scripts/regen_all.py --write`) through the integrator AFTER WS3 merges, or hand the hunk to WS3. Line regions are disjoint (WS1 :115-130 vs WS3 :203-234) so textual merge is clean, but WS3's edits shift line numbers below :130 is false / above :200 is true — WS1's citation of :205-234 in its REQUIRES will drift once WS3 deletes the coverage re-run. Merge WS3 first, then WS1 with integrator re-anchoring the hunk by content, not line number.

cross_unit_conflicts.resource: docs/internals/engineering-principles.md enforcement map (SOFT — four units each append one row/section)
cross_unit_conflicts.resolution: All appends are append-only (no existing lines touched). Serialize at merge time only: integrator resolves the trivial same-region append conflicts in merge order; units develop in parallel. Each unit's row must be self-contained; WS7 CITES WS6's row (does not add one), and WS6 must receive the DC5 verbatim row text — including WS7's five test IDs — in its dispatch brief even though WS7 merges two waves later.

cross_unit_conflicts.resource: PackManifest.derived_from seam (INTERFACE dependency: WS6 reads the field WS5 creates; WS6's tests need WS5's reseal_manifest derived_from passthrough)
cross_unit_conflicts.resolution: WS5 merges before WS6 (both digests agree). Interface-first parallel DEVELOPMENT is sound: the seam is fully specced (frozen dataclass {pack, seam, version, sha}, PackManifest.derived_from: X | None = None, parse_manifest optional-key/loud-SpecInvalid, reseal passthrough), so WS6 can build in wave 1 on a branch — but it cannot self-verify green until rebased onto merged WS5, so its verify+merge is wave 2. wave_plan.rationale: Five units are pairwise file-disjoint except two managed overlaps: ci.yml (WS1/WS3 — disjoint line regions, integrator merges WS3 first and re-anchors WS1's hunk) and engineering-principles.md (append-only, integrator serializes). WS5 is fully disjoint from everything in the wave. Interface-first parallelism extends to WS6 development because its input seam (frozen dataclass fields, PackManifest.derived_from default-None, parse_manifest semantics, reseal passthrough) is completely specced in both digests — but ONLY after the DerivedFrom/PackDerivation naming contradiction is settled at dispatch; WS6 cannot self-verify green until WS5 merges, so it does not merge in this wave. Integrator merge order: WS3 -> WS1 -> WS2 -> WS4 -> WS5. Wave close-out: run `python scripts/regen_all.py --write` once (the only regen point — WS5 is the sole regen-needed unit), wire merge_generated `ensure` into regen_all (the WS1/WS2 cross-wire neither unit owns), verify the ledger gate is green at HEAD, full CI on the merged head before wave 2.

WS6's tests require WS5's merged PackManifest.derived_from and the reseal derived_from passthrough (its own top hazard: WS6-before-WS5 makes HEAD red with AttributeError/parse refusals). If WS6 developed in wave 1, this wave is rebase + green-verify + merge — short. WS7 must NOT start from a stub here: it shares pack_declarations.py and the test file including fixture helpers WS6 rewrites; interface-first is not sound for same-module co-tenants.

Hard-serialized behind WS6 (same module, same test file, fixture-helper generalization, and its agreement invariant is only total once WS6's no-silent-tiebreak law is in the tree). WS7 builds on merged WS6, cites (never restates) WS6's enforcement row. No regen needed. Wave close-out: full CI, then the sweep is done — the harxhar-clean adopt-mode migration and any env wheel refresh remain HUMAN steps gated on run-13 fleet harvest.

global_hazards: RUN-13 LIVE RELAY: WS5 changes state/pack_sweep.py _semantic. If absent derived_from does not compare exactly equal between recipe-fresh and on-disk manifests, every existing pack reads stale on the next gate tick and mass-reseals — revoking harxhar-clean's live receipts the moment a fix wheel ships. Two protections: (a) an explicit regression test that a pre-sweep manifest without derived_from round-trips _semantic-equal; (b) NO cluster/demo env wheel refresh until the run-13 fleets harvest (standing directive in the relay memory). Merging to main is safe; installing is not.

COUPLED-DEPLOY TRAP (WS5+WS6 vs harxhar-clean): once a wheel carrying WS6 lands in the lab env, multi-candidate compose REFUSES (SpecInvalid) until the human runs program-init adopt to stamp derived_from into the rv manifest — exactly the audit_preflight site where finding 1 fired. The migration cannot precede the wheel (the verb ships in it), so there is a planned refusal window; sequence wheel-install -> immediate program-init adopt in the same human session, after run-13 harvest. Also: adopt mode must not byte-change the SIGNED rv_audit.py (sha 8a0ccccc...) or all five section sign-offs are invalidated (finding-1 blast radius).

MAIN-STAYS-SHIPPABLE vs the strict ledger gate: WS1's test_regen_debt_ledger.py lands red if any ledger row is genuinely unpaid at HEAD without a RED strict-xfail marking. WS1's agent must reconcile the ledger against live HEAD in the same commit the gate lands — pre-verify at dispatch, not at merge.

WS2 MANIFEST OVER-INCLUSION: any partially generated file in FULLY_GENERATED_PATTERNS makes merges silently discard theirs-side hand-written content that regen never restores. Specific trap this very sweep: docs/primitives/*.md are hand-authored bodies with GENERATED frontmatter (WS5 adds docs/primitives/program-init.md) — they must stay out of the merge=generated set; only docs/primitives/README.md (index) is fully generated. The pin test must encode this exclusion, not just the inclusion list.

BRANCH-PROTECTION CONTEXTS: WS3 (and WS1's ci.yml hunk) must keep job-level check names byte-stable; a renamed required context blocks every subsequent PR in the sweep including the integrator's own merges.

SEAM-NAME CONTRADICTION: WS5 exports `DerivedFrom`, WS6 requires `PackDerivation` (per the domain-packs.md:587-600 ruling). Unresolved, WS6's wave-1 development builds against a phantom symbol and the whole interface-first plan degenerates to strict sequencing. Settle before dispatch.

REGISTRY COUNT SHIFT: WS5's regen bumps the registry 169->170. Any frozen count literal or pinning test (WS4's new frozen-count lint, test_prose_primitive_count.py, the '167 primitives' allowlist) must be live-derived or reconciled in the same wave, or the wave-1 merge head goes red on its own regen.

Batch B (B1-B5) is deliberately unowned (pends maintainer rulings) — correct, but B3 (formalize the handoff-package model) is effectively being re-executed ad hoc by this very swarm; the integrator should bank this sweep's disjointness table as the B3 precedent.

No unit owns a post-wave-1 verification that the WS2 merge driver actually behaves on this sweep's own merges (the integrator's WS5 regen commit is the first real fully-generated-file merge the driver could mediate) — a one-off integrator smoke (`git config merge.generated.driver` present + a synthetic conflict) closes the loop the premortems left open.
